name: STEMI - Posterior
identifyBy: Mirror image in standard leads: dominant R in V1-V2, horizontal ST depression V1-V3, upright T waves
Direct confirmation: ST elevation >=0.5mm in posterior leads V7-V9
Often co-presents with inferior STEMI (RCA or LCx territory)
Tall broad R wave in V1 >=0.04s (equivalent to posterior Q wave)
Most commonly missed STEMI - requires posterior leads to confirm
management: Standard 12-lead - look for ST depression + tall R in V1-V3
Apply posterior leads V7-V9 if posterior STEMI suspected
GTN with caution - assess for RV co-involvement
Posterior STEMI has same reperfusion priority as anterior STEMI
pearls: The 'dominant R in V1' should trigger suspicion for posterior STEMI - this is a Q-wave equivalent
Posterior STEMI is the most commonly missed STEMI territory - always consider when V1-V3 show ST depression + tall R
V7 is placed at the left posterior axillary line, V8 at left scapular tip, V9 at left paravertebral
ST depression >=0.5mm in V1-V3 + tall R wave = apply posterior leads immediately
differentials: RBBB (tall R V1, but different morphology)
RV hypertrophy (tall R V1, right axis deviation)

qaNotes: Ischaemia/STEMI morphology retained from source content; verify affected leads in multi-lead view.
